summary: Operator-requested hardening: degradation visuals must stay inside the visible sprite. New world.block_opaque_mask(block_id) returns a cached BitMap of a tile texture's opaque pixels (art or generated fallback; null for air/unknown). The FQ-08 mining crack overlay keeps its per-cell deterministic seed/RNG sequence but rasterizes each crack segment pixel by pixel through the mask, so thin tree trunks, leaves, bushes, and torches only crack where their pixels exist; solid tiles are unaffected. Town Hall damage overlay and enemy hurt visuals audited — already sprite-bounded, no change. New smoke check fq08_crack_mask_inside_sprite; suite 183 -> 184, all green.
scope: Single hardening item on operator request (follow-up to FQ-08 damage visuals and FQ-09R unified trees). Implementation commit e5cf23c plus this evidence-only commit; pushed to origin/main on explicit request.
next_action: FQ-09S: skill tree visual treatment pass.
